questions.question: nohup runs the given command with hangup signals ignored, so that the command can continue running in the background after you log out.
questions.explanation: Correct. nohup runs the given command with hangup signals ignored, so that the command can continue running in the background after you log out.

questions.explanation: Correct. If standard error is a terminal, it is normally redirected to the same file descriptor as the (possibly-redirected) standard output.

questions.question: nohup does not automatically put the command it runs in the background; you must do that explicitly, by ending the command line with an ‘ & ’.
questions.explanation: Correct. nohup does not automatically put the command it runs in the background; you must do that explicitly, by ending the command line with an ‘ & ’.

questions.question: nohup does not not alter the niceness of command ; use nice for that, e.g., ‘ nohup nice command ’.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. nohup does not automatically put the command it runs in the background; you must do that explicitly, by ending the command line with an ‘ & ’.

questions.explanation: Correct. Also, nohup does not alter the niceness of command ; use nice for that, e.g., ‘ nohup nice command ’.
